name: TON
info: A standard Timer On-Delay (TON) block. The output turns true only after the input has been true for the specified delay period. Resets instantly if the input turns false.

meta.author: diego.arcos@jborrell.com. OsoLogic®
meta.desc: A standard Timer On-Delay (TON) block. The output turns true only after the input has been true for the specified delay period. Resets instantly if the input turns false.

name: TOF
info: A standard Timer Off-Delay (TOF) block. The output turns true immediately with the input. The output remains true for the specified delay after the input turns false.

meta.author: diego.arcos@jborrell.com. OsoLogic®
meta.desc: A standard Timer Off-Delay (TOF) block. The output turns true immediately with the input. The output remains true for the specified delay after the input turns false.

name: TP
info: A standard Pulse Timer (TP) block. On a rising edge of the input (false to true), the output turns true for a fixed duration, then turns false. Subsequent triggers during the pulse are ignored.

meta.author: diego.arcos@jborrell.com. OsoLogic®
meta.desc: A standard Pulse Timer (TP) block. On a rising edge of the input (false to true), the output turns true for a fixed duration, then turns false. Subsequent triggers during the pulse are ignored.

name: Universal Notifier
info: A centralized notification hub. Receives a message with topic, payload, and priority (1=Info, 2=Warning, 3=Critical) and routes it to the appropriate channels (Log File, Email, Telegram).

meta.author: diego.arcos@jborrell.com. OsoLogic®
meta.desc: A centralized notification hub. Receives a message with topic, payload, and priority (1=Info, 2=Warning, 3=Critical) and routes it to the appropriate channels (Log File, Email, Telegram).
meta.keywords: plc, notify, notification, alarm, email, telegram, log, borrell-plc

name: Read IO
info: Filters the main I/O bus for a specific label. You have to connect the '[CORE] HAL' bus to the input of this node. It will only output messages (RBE) for the specified I/O point label.

name: Write IO
info: Writes a single boolean (true/false) to the specified 'label'. If 'use_fixed_value' is checked, it sends the 'value' from properties. If unchecked, it sends the incoming 'msg.payload'.

name: Write IOs
info: Writes a batch of boolean commands. If 'use_fixed_commands' is checked, it sends the 'commands' array from properties. If unchecked, it sends the incoming 'msg.payload' (must be an array). The array is split into individual messages.

name: Read IOs
info:
Filters the I/O bus for a specific group of labels. Set the 'labels' to allow in the node's Environment Properties (as a JSON array of strings, e.g., ["Sensor 1", "Sensor 2"]).
You have to connect the '[CORE] HAL' bus to the input of this node.
This subflow listens to the 'Bus-in' link. It acts as a simple filter and only allows messages (msg.topic + msg.payload) to pass if their 'topic' is included in the configured 'labels' array. Messages not in the list are blocked.
